claims.claim_text: A late-2024 quantitative trading-firm incident was caused by deploying a standard LLM to generate migration scripts.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 1, sentence 1
claims.anchor_text: In late 2024, a leading quantitative trading firm attempted to automate its internal data pipeline migrations by deploying a state-of-the-art standard large language model.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Teams that let a standard LLM generate and run migration code without strong review can introduce subtle but serious production errors.
claims.rationale: This is a dated, specific war story about a real organization type, but no citable primary source is identified.

claims.claim_text: The generated migration created a cascading row-level lock on the primary transaction database.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 1, sentence 2
claims.anchor_text: When the script executed autonomously in production, it created a cascading row-level lock on the primary transaction database.
claims.suggested_rewrite: If generated migration code runs in production without safeguards, it can trigger severe database contention or other failure modes.
claims.rationale: The incident mechanics are presented as a real event, but no primary incident report is given.

claims.claim_text: The outage halted trading for 22 minutes and caused an estimated $14 million loss.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 1, sentence 3
claims.anchor_text: This halted all high-frequency trades for twenty-two minutes, resulting in an estimated fourteen million dollar loss.
claims.suggested_rewrite: In a trading environment, a production pipeline failure can cause substantial financial damage very quickly.
claims.rationale: This is a fake-precise loss and duration claim with no attributable source.

claims.claim_text: Daniel Kahneman popularized the System 1/System 2 framework, with System 1 as fast and intuitive and System 2 as slower and more deliberative.
claims.span_hint: section: The Shift from System 1 to System 2 Thinking, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The cited page directly describes Kahneman's book and its System 1/System 2 definitions.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI reported that its reasoning models think before answering and improve with more test-time compute.
claims.span_hint: section: The Shift from System 1 to System 2 Thinking, paragraph 2; section: The Mechanics of Test-Time Compute, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: OpenAI's release page explicitly says o1 uses chain-of-thought reasoning and that performance improves with more time spent thinking.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI introduced a `reasoning_effort` parameter to control how long a reasoning model thinks before answering.
claims.span_hint: section: The Mechanics of Test-Time Compute, paragraph 2
claims.rationale: OpenAI's developer announcement directly introduces the `reasoning_effort` API parameter.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI o3-mini offers low, medium, and high reasoning-effort settings.
claims.span_hint: section: The Mechanics of Test-Time Compute, paragraph 3 and code sample
claims.rationale: The o3-mini release page explicitly lists three reasoning-effort options: low, medium, and high.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI released the o1 series in 2024 and reported that more inference-time compute improved reasoning performance.
claims.anchor_text: In late 2024, OpenAI released the o1 model series, which demonstrated that scaling inference compute could yield performance gains comparable to scaling pre-training compute, fundamentally shifting how hardware demands are forecasted in data centers.
claims.suggested_rewrite: In 2024, OpenAI released the o1 model series and reported that reasoning performance improved when the model spent more time thinking at inference time.
claims.rationale: The release and test-time-compute finding are supportable, but the hardware-forecasting consequence is not directly backed on the primary pages.

claims.claim_text: DeepSeek R1 used cold-start training before reinforcement learning and reported strong early-2025 math, code, and reasoning benchmark results.
claims.rationale: The DeepSeek-R1 model card directly describes cold-start data before RL and includes the benchmark table published in January 2025.

claims.claim_text: Hidden reasoning tokens can account for up to 95% of total completion tokens on complex queries.
claims.anchor_text: Hidden reasoning tokens can account for up to 95% of the total completion tokens generated in highly complex algorithmic queries, drastically altering the unit economics of AI API consumption for enterprise applications.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Hidden reasoning tokens can dominate total completion-token usage on difficult queries, which can materially change API cost calculations for enterprise applications.
claims.rationale: The exact 95% figure is presented as fact but no authoritative source is identified.

claims.claim_text: Early reasoning models sometimes produced more than 30,000 internal tokens and over two minutes of latency before visible output.
claims.anchor_text: During internal beta testing, some early reasoning models generated internal chains of thought exceeding 30,000 tokens before producing a single word of user-facing output, resulting in absolute latencies exceeding two full minutes per query.
claims.suggested_rewrite: During early reasoning-model testing, some systems exhibited very long internal reasoning traces and noticeably higher latency before the first visible output.
claims.rationale: The token and latency figures are highly specific but unattributed.

further_reading.title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful background for the System 1/System 2 analogy used throughout the module.

further_reading.title: Learning to reason with LLMs
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary OpenAI release explaining train-time vs test-time compute and the behavior of reasoning models.

further_reading.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary paper for one of the major reasoning-model families referenced in the module.

notes: Excluded Mermaid diagrams, code blocks, quiz prompts, and exercise steps per instructions. The main allowlist gap is OpenAI platform-doc coverage for prompting guidance, reasoning-token billing/metadata, and temperature support on reasoning models.
